data.statement: Comment-only or whitespace-only edits to .py files must not flag linked memories needs_review. In invalidate._check_path, when the blob hash differs and the path ends in .py, call the token-equivalence check (core/semantic.py: python_source_is_semantically_equivalent) via a helper reading both blobs with blob_content; if equivalent, return status 'reanchored' with the new hash instead of 'changed'. Fail-safe: any decode/tokenize failure degrades to flagging as before.
data.why_it_matters: EVALUATION.md measured 100% spurious needs_review on cosmetic edits; tokenizer comparison (dropping COMMENT/NL, keeping INDENT) is the fix — git diff -w was rejected because indentation is semantic in Python.

data.statement: When a memory transitions to active — via 'roco status <id> activate' after a needs_review confirmation, or via evidence-based auto-promotion from provisional — the store must actually re-capture what 'verified now' means: memory.reverify(conn, repo_root, memory_id) re-reads the current HEAD blob hash of every linked path and bumps last_verified_at. If activation only flips the status column, the next staleness refresh compares against the old stored hash and re-flags needs_review forever.
data.why_it_matters: Without reverify at both call sites (lifecycle.change_status when new_status=='active', and evidence.attach_evidence's auto-promotion), 'I checked, it's still true' records nothing to verify against — the re-flag loop never breaks.

data.statement: CLI commands must never leak raw tracebacks for expected failures. On a repo with zero commits, core/git.py head_tree() raises bare CalledProcessError (git exit 128) — guard with a has_commits() helper raising ValidationError('has no commits yet'), reused by init_repo() which returns a 'warning' field. cli/app.py main() needs a last-resort catch-all around args.func(args) printing '{cmd}: internal error' with a ROBO_CORTEX_DEBUG=1 hint.
data.why_it_matters: retrieve/search/affected on a freshly git-init'd repo, and merge with a missing input file, crashed with raw Python tracebacks instead of one-line errors.
